divergences.description: Agent B classifies CLAUDE.md as BLOCKER (production App IDs and Installation IDs should not appear in the first public commit). Agent C classifies it as IMPORTANT (App IDs are not secrets, visible in GitHub UI, and the file provides development value). Agent A groups it with internal docs as a combined BLOCKER. All agree on remediation (sanitize, not delete); the disagreement is purely whether sanitization must happen before or shortly after the repo goes public.

rationale: All three agents now converge on the core blocker set (hardcoded Modal domains, CLI API URLs, SECURITY.md, license metadata, internal planning docs), the 19-issue scope, the branding vs lock-in distinction, CLAUDE.md sanitize-not-delete remediation, auth rate limiting as CRITICAL, remediation sequencing, and the release contract as the top strategic question. Agent A produced the cleanest convergence by adopting all valid findings and declaring full alignment. Agent C maintained the sharpest analytical rigor with principled severity calibration and the most useful open questions. The sole remaining divergence is a severity judgment call on CLAUDE.md that does not affect the remediation action itself.
